Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Ames Experts Available for Artemis II Flight Test Interviews

NASA’s Ames Research Center in Silicon Valley invites media to interview local subject matter experts on Friday, March 27 from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. ahead of the agency sending astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years with the Artemis II flight test. NASA teams are gearing up...

NASA Ames Research Center is positioning its subject matter experts as key resources for media coverage of Artemis II, signaling institutional commitment to public engagement around lunar return missions.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

5 Airlines With The Largest Widebody Fleets In North America

All five are based in the United States.

U.S. carriers' dominance in widebody fleets reflects their control of premium long-haul markets and international route networks across North America.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Sets Coverage for First Artemis Crewed Mission Around Moon

A variety of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for NASA’s Artemis II mission around the Moon will stream online. The agency is targeting no earlier than Wednesday, April 1, for the test flight during a two-hour window that opens at 6:24 p.m. EDT, with additional launch opportunities through...

NASA's Artemis II lunar mission represents a critical validation of deep-space human spaceflight capabilities essential for sustainable aerospace exploration architecture.

See NASA →

PAI
Safety Mar 25, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation safety from investigating accidents post-incident to identifying and mitigating risks before they occur, fundamentally changing how the industry prioritizes prevention.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Space Force weighs launch alternatives as Vulcan faces potential months-long grounding

Officials explore shifting missions, extending satellites already in orbit

The Space Force's contingency planning to redistribute Vulcan launches across multiple providers signals growing reliance on competitive launch redundancy rather than single-vendor dependency.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Delta Air Lines Continues Los Angeles Lead, Schedules More Flights To Florida This Winter

Delta is adding more Florida flying from LAX as its battle with United intensifies.

Delta's capacity expansion on the LAX-Florida route signals intensifying competition for high-yield leisure and business traffic during peak winter travel season.

See Simple Flying →

Corporate Mar 25, 2026

JetBlue Considers Selling Itself To A Major US Carrier, Weighs How Washington Will React

JetBlue's exploration of a potential sale to rival airlines has sparked intense speculation and antitrust scrutiny.

JetBlue's sale exploration signals that even established carriers may struggle profitably in current market conditions, forcing consolidation despite regulatory headwinds.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to systematically document why selected controls are adequate despite remaining residual risks, creating accountability for risk acceptance decisions.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Up To 22-Hour Flights: United Airlines' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Discover the airline's most extended nonstop and one-stop services, some of which are quite controversial.

Ultra-long routes demand crew rest protocols, specialized aircraft maintenance schedules, and passenger comfort innovations that will reshape operational costs and cabin design standards.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

The US Airlines With The Most Spacious Premium Economy Seats In 2026

This article will provide the reader with an in-depth look at the US airlines with the most spacious Premium Economy Seats In 2026.

US carriers are expanding premium economy pitch and width to compete for mid-cabin revenue as international long-haul markets increasingly demand affordable lie-flat alternatives.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Sets Coverage for Artemis II Moon Mission

Editor’s note: NASA will continuously update this Artemis II briefings and mission events page throughout prelaunch, launch, and mission activities. NASA will provide live coverage of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for the agency’s upcoming Artemis II crewed test flight around the Moon....

NASA's continuous live coverage strategy for Artemis II demonstrates the agency's commitment to public transparency while managing complex mission communications across multiple phases.

See NASA →

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strike accidents persist because pilot overconfidence in reaction time masks the physical impossibility of avoiding obstacles during normal operations, making proactive route planning essential.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Artemis Moon Tree Dedicated in Honor of Mary W. Jackson

On March 18, 2026, students, staff, and members of NASA’s Langley Research Center gathered at Mary W. Jackson Elementary School in Hampton to celebrate the dedication of a remarkable addition to the campus – an Artemis Moon Tree. Although formally dedicated on this day, the loblolly pine had...

NASA's Moon Tree program symbolically connects human spaceflight heritage with STEM education, inspiring the next generation of aerospace engineers and scientists.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Why Emirates Doesn't Want The Boeing 737 MAX

The airline has little interest in this popular narrowbody.

Emirates' strategic focus on wide-body aircraft for long-haul routes and hub connectivity makes the 737 MAX operationally misaligned with their network model, not a rejection of the aircraft itself.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Southwest Airlines Passenger Blasts New Policy After Being Required To Buy An Extra Seat

One passenger's ordeal at the airport in March 2026 raises questions about Southwest's 'Customer of Size' policy and its impact on travelers.

Southwest's enforcement of seating policies on full flights reveals operational tension between capacity constraints and passenger accommodation standards that could reshape industry accessibility practices.

See Simple Flying →

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise abatement procedures must navigate complex Bay Area airspace constraints while managing residential proximity and terrain challenges.

US House passes bill to clear way for overland supersonic flights

The US House of Representatives passed legislation aimed at opening the skies to civil supersonic flight over land,…

Regulatory approval pathway for overland supersonic operations could reshape domestic route economics and aircraft development timelines for manufacturers.

eVTOL Mar 25, 2026

Eve flies eVTOL prototype for Brazilian president in high-profile test milestone

Eve Air Mobility flew its full-scale engineering prototype for Brazilian authorities, including President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva,…

Eve's presidential demonstration signals government backing critical for regulatory approval and future eVTOL commercialization in Latin America.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

How Many McDonnell Douglas MD-11 Aircraft Are In Service?

The jet has mostly disappeared from our skies.

MD-11s have largely transitioned to cargo operations, extending their economic viability despite passenger service decline.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 25, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-airspace systems offers near-term capacity gains while sustainable aviation technologies mature, requiring coordinated investment across all stakeholders.

See MDPI →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Names Jennifer Lyons Acting Launch Services Program Manager

NASA has selected Jennifer Lyons as acting program manager for the agency’s Launch Services Program (LSP) based at Kennedy Space Center in Florida. In this role, Lyons will lead NASA’s acquisition and management of domestic commercial launch services for science and robotic exploration missions...

NASA's launch services acquisition strategy now prioritizes commercial providers as the default option for science missions, reshaping competition and contract opportunities in the domestic launch sector.

See NASA →

Drones Mar 25, 2026

BRINC Unveils Guardian, Launching the Next Era of Drone as First Responder

Guardian delivers 24/7 operations, unmatched imaging, Starlink connectivity, and more to help public safety agencies

BRINC's Starlink integration enables drones to maintain persistent connectivity in remote areas where traditional networks fail, fundamentally expanding first-responder operational range.

See sUAS News →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Office of Space Commerce releases mission authorization proposal

The Office of Space Commerce has rolled out its proposal for a “light touch” approach to mission authorization, the latest attempt in an effort to regulate new commercial space applications.

The lighter regulatory framework aims to accelerate commercial space venture approvals while maintaining safety oversight, potentially reducing time-to-market for aerospace companies entering the commercial space sector.

See SpaceNews →

Budget drone models now compete on capability rather than price alone, reshaping procurement strategies across commercial and industrial sectors.

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

NAVI AI Launches Pilot Training Platform

Navi AI has unveiled a new AI-driven platform designed to enhance pilot training by automating post-flight debriefs.

AI-automated debriefs could significantly reduce training costs and standardize instruction quality across pilot development programs.

See AVweb →

Safety Mar 25, 2026

Pilot Shares What It’s Like To Perform A Go-Around During Landing

A pilot reveals the reality of go-arounds—why they happen, how they’re flown, and why abandoning a landing is often the safest decision.

Go-arounds represent sound aeronautical decision-making rather than operational failures, with modern traffic management systems increasingly accommodating these safety-prioritized maneuvers.

See Simple Flying →

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

Textron Aviation To Showcase Full Range Of Aircraft At Sun ’n Fun

Textron Aviation will display Cessna, Beechcraft, and Pipistrel aircraft together for the first time at Sun ’n Fun 2026.

Textron's unified aircraft showcase signals a strategic pivot toward integrated brand positioning rather than maintaining separate product line identities.

See AVweb →

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

Cirrus posts strong 2025 with 691 SR Series deliveries, 106 Vision Jets

Cirrus Aircraft reported it delivered 691 SR Series piston aircraft and a record 106 Vision Jets in 2025,…

Cirrus's Vision Jet momentum—106 deliveries in 2025—signals accelerating market acceptance of single-engine jets for personal aviation, reshaping the premium general-aviation segment.

See AeroTime Hub →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Webb Captures Saturn in Infrared

Saturn’s icy rings glow in this infrared view from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope released on March 25, 2026. Combining this image with another that NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope captured – also released on the same day – provides scientists with a richer, more layered understanding of the gas...

Webb's infrared Saturn imagery demonstrates advanced remote sensing capabilities applicable to atmospheric characterization and celestial navigation systems for future space missions.

See NASA →

General Aviation Mar 25, 2026

Study Unveils Redevelopment Concepts For Burke Lakefront Airport

A new economic study outlines two potential mixed-use redevelopment plans for Burke Lakefront Airport, part of Mayor Justin Bibb’s effort to close the city-operated airport.

Mixed-use redevelopment proposals signal a shift from aviation to real estate monetization for underperforming urban airports, reflecting broader trends in repurposing prime waterfront property.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Terran Orbital Introduces New Star Tracker Product Line at SATSHOW 2026

IRVINE, Calif., March 25, 2026 — Terran Orbital Corporation, a Lockheed Martin company, today announced the introduction of its new star tracker product line at SATSHOW 2026, expanding the company’s […]

Terran Orbital's new star tracker line positions Lockheed Martin to capture growing demand for precision attitude-determination systems in the expanding satellite constellation market.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

Spirit Airlines vs. Frontier Airlines: Comparing The 2 Ultra-Low-Cost Carriers' Seat Pitch And Carry-On Bag Restrictions

The two airlines offer a similar experience, but have slight variations.

Spirit and Frontier's nearly identical ultra-low-cost models highlight how standardized industry practices have become, limiting differentiation opportunities for carriers competing in this segment.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Webb, Hubble Share Most Comprehensive View of Saturn to Date

NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope and Hubble Space Telescope have teamed up to capture new views of Saturn, revealing the planet in strikingly different ways. Observing in complementary wavelengths of light, the two space observatories provide scientists with a richer, more layered understanding of...

Multi-wavelength satellite observations demonstrate complementary data collection methods applicable to Earth-monitoring aerospace systems and remote sensing technologies.

See NASA →

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA-JAXA’s XRISM Telescope Clocks Hot Wind of Galaxy M82

For the first time, astronomers have directly measured the speed of superheated gas billowing from a cauldron of stellar activity at the heart of M82.

Direct measurement of galactic wind dynamics advances understanding of high-energy astrophysical phenomena relevant to aerospace instrument development and space science mission objectives.

See NASA →

Commercial Mar 25, 2026

FAA Clears Higher Takeoff Weights For Boeing 787-9, -10

The FAA has approved higher takeoff weights for the Boeing 787-9 and 787-10, giving operators more flexibility to carry additional payload or extend range on long-haul routes.

Higher certified weights improve fleet economics by enabling carriers to optimize payload-range tradeoffs without requiring aircraft modifications.
